mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.16%), the Fed's sustained ultra-accommodative stance lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and pushes investors into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains near zero.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) easing cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 12-month window while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, or SPX exhibits negative mean 20d returns during the ZIRP period at p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month increase in UNRATE decelerates to 0.1 percentage points or less for two consecutive months after a prior rapid rise, it signals the labor market is bottoming, which forward-anticipates a broader economic recovery and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE MoM increase <= 0.1pp for 2 consecutive months following a period of increases > 0.3pp
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.7% in Aug 2009 after steep 2008-2009 climb
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or the deceleration pattern precedes a renewed UNRATE acceleration of >0.3pp within 3 months in a majority of historical analogs.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in INDPRO reflects manufacturing output recovery and rising forward corporate earnings expectations, which continuously translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as the industrial trend persists.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=95.97 showing sequential improvement from 2009 trough
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month momentum and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 across any 2-year rolling window, or returns are flat/negative despite consistent INDPRO increases for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint improvement in HOUST (housing starts) and RSAFS (retail sales) signals a broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery; the interaction of both series turning positive simultaneously is a stronger confirmation of durable demand recovery than either alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month % change > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month % change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=581.0 and RSAFS=351418 showing tentative 2009 stabilization
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly triggered, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or insignificant (p>0.05) in more than 45% of historical instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone (interaction IC not exceeding max single-series IC).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned at the zero lower bound while CPIAUCSL shows persistent disinflation (low or falling year-over-year inflation), markets price in liquidity-trap deflation risk where conventional monetary easing loses traction, producing negative or muted S&P 500 returns over the next week as risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.16, CPIAUCSL=214.469 amid post-crisis disinflation concerns in 2009
regime_hint: zero lower bound liquidity trap / deflation risk regime
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods meeting the joint trigger condition, or the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL YoY change stabilizes above 1% without corresponding market improvement.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the pace of PAYEMS job losses combined with UNRATE growth slowing simultaneously indicates the labor market inflection point is approaching, and this joint signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as markets front-run the employment recovery.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly decline shrinks by >30% from its trough pace AND UNRATE MoM increase <= 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=131223 thousand with decelerating job losses through mid-2009
regime_hint: labor market inflection during recession recovery
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns fail to exceed unconditional baseline mean by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05) when both conditions are triggered, or the signal reverses sign in more than one of three historical recession-recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, which is continuously priced by equity markets as a leading indicator of near-term corporate revenue strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=351418 million showing incremental monthly stabilization in mid-2009
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS MoM growth and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window, or the sign of the relationship flips across more than two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO year-over-year growth crosses from negative to positive territory after a deep contraction, it marks a confirmed transition from recession to expansion in industrial activity; in this early-recovery regime the crossing event drives strong positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as cyclical earnings expectations re-rate higher.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY change crosses from negative to positive for the first time after a contraction exceeding -5% YoY
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=95.97 recovering from 2009 trough amid GDPC1=12892.5 signaling recession bottoming
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery following deep industrial recession
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following the crossing event are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.05) in more than 40% of historical crossing episodes since 1960.
